Package org.key_project.util.collection

Class Immutables

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.util.collection.Immutables
public final class Immutables extends Object
This class is a collection of methods that operate on immutable collections, in particular ImmutableSets and ImmutableLists. This class cannot be instantiated.

  • Method Details

    • isDuplicateFree

      public static <T> boolean isDuplicateFree(ImmutableList<T> list)
      Checks whether an immutable list is free of duplicates. A list has a duplicate if it during iteration it visits two objects o1 and o2 such that o1==null ? o2 == null : o1.equals(o2) is true. null may appear in the list. The implementation uses a hash set internally and thus runs in O(n).
      Parameters:
      list - any list, must not be null
      Returns:
      true iff every

    • removeDuplicates

      public static <T> ImmutableList<T> removeDuplicates(ImmutableList<T> list)
      Removes duplicate entries from an immutable list. A list has a duplicate if it during iteration it visits two objects o1 and o2 such that o1==null ? o2 == null : o1.equals(o2) is true. null may appear in the list. If an element occurs duplicated, its first (in order of iteration) occurrence is kept, while later occurrences are removeed. If a list iterates "a", "b", "a" in this order, removeDuplicates returns a list iterating "a", "b". The implementation uses a hash set internally and thus runs in O(n). It reuses as much created datastructure as possible. In particular, if the list is already duplicate-fre, it does not allocate new memory (well, only temporarily) and returns the argument. Sidenote: Would that not make a nice KeY-Verification condition? Eat your own dogfood.
      Parameters:
      list - any list, must not be null
      Returns:
      a duplicate-free version of the argument, never null

    • concatDuplicateFreeLists

      public static <T> ImmutableList<T> concatDuplicateFreeLists(ImmutableList<T> l1, ImmutableList<? extends T> l2)

    • createSetFrom

      public static <T> ImmutableSet<T> createSetFrom(Iterable<T> iterable)

    • createListFrom

      public static <T> ImmutableList<T> createListFrom(Iterable<T> iterable)
      Returns an immutable list consisting of the elements of the given iterable collection. The iteration order of the result is identical to that of the argument.
      Parameters:
      iterable - the collection to iterate through to obtain the elements for the resulting list

    • filter

      public static <T> ImmutableList<T> filter(ImmutableList<T> ts, Predicate<T> predicate)
      Returns an immutable list consisting of the elements of the list that match the given predicate.
      Parameters:
      ts - non-null immutable list.
      predicate - a non-interfering, stateless predicate to apply to each element to determine if it should be included

    • map

      public static <T, R> ImmutableList<R> map(ImmutableList<T> ts, Function<T,R> function)
      Returns an immutable list consisting of the results of applying the given function to the elements of the list.
      Type Parameters:
      R - The element type of the result list
      Parameters:
      ts - ts non-null immutable list.
      function - a non-interfering, stateless function to apply to each element
      Returns:
      the mapped list of the same length as this
